══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  SECTION 3 — CLAUDE ANALYSIS NOTES
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  This section is written for Claude to read at the start of a research session.
  It summarises what happened, what looks suspicious, and what to try next.
  All recommendations reference the GRA document set (reconstruction_algorithms.md,
  genealogical_constraints.md) where relevant.


  ── Issues detected
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ISSUE 1:
    High-frequency surnames detected: 'graham' (284), 'cassidy' (206),
    'wray' (182), 'gallagher' (147), 'mccadden' (125). In a small
    townland community, dominant surnames create many same-surname
    candidate pairs. Without Splink term-frequency (TF) adjustment, a
    surname match carries the same discriminating weight regardless of
    frequency, inflating match probabilities for ambiguous pairs
    (reconstruction_algorithms.md §4.4).

  ISSUE 2:
    190 pairs scored in [0.80–0.85), just below the auto-commit threshold
    of 0.85. These were queued as proposals rather than committed. If
    manual review of these proposals shows they are mostly correct
    linkages, the threshold may be conservatively high for this dataset.
    If they are mostly incorrect, the model needs more training data.

  ISSUE 3:
    542 pairs scored in [0.30–0.35), just above the suppression floor.
    These low-confidence proposals may represent transcription-damaged
    records or extended-family members with similar names. They warrant
    careful review and are likely flag candidates rather than accepts.

  ISSUE 4:
    16 merged pair(s) have birth year delta > 5 years (max: 74 years).
    The GC03 census age drift tolerance is ±3 years for 1901↔1911 and ±4
    for 1901↔1926. Deltas above 5 suggest informant error or a genuine
    mismatch. These merges should be reviewed and potentially rolled
    back.


  ── What looks good
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ✓ Feature matrix (3881 persons across 3 census sources) is adequate for
  Splink EM parameter estimation.
  ✓ Birth year null rate: 2% — good feature coverage.
  ✓ Place null rate: 0% — most persons have a resolved townland.
  ✓ 264 persons auto-committed at mean score 0.918.
  ✓ 3291 pairs proposed for researcher review.
  ✓ Place match rate among merged pairs: 100% — good spatial coherence.
  Merges are consistent with GC22 geographical coherence.

  ── Recommended actions
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ACTION 1:
    Enable Splink term-frequency adjustment for surname_norm. In
    _build_settings(), configure the JaroWinklerAtThresholds comparison
    for surname_norm with term_frequency_adjustments=True. The frequency
    table is computed automatically from the feature DataFrame by
    Splink's EM training.

  ACTION 2:
    In the update-knowledge session, review the 190 near-commit proposals
    first. If ≥80% are correct, consider lowering AUTO_COMMIT_THRESHOLD
    to 0.82 on a trial basis. Document the decision in ROADMAP.md.

  ACTION 3:
    In the update-knowledge session, flag merged persons with birth year
    delta > 5 for researcher review. Cross-reference with household
    membership (GC16 couple co-residency, GC15 parent-child co-residency)
    to triangulate whether the age discrepancy is likely informant error
    or a different person.
